subject: refactor(structure): unify cell selection with global selectedCell
body:
Replaced the parallel structureSelection state system with the same
global selectedCell used by all other tabs. Section context (which
row/column a cell belongs to) is now derived via cellToSection() instead
of stored separately.

What changed:
- Removed internal CellGrid component (~200 lines), structureSelection
  state, lastInternalCell ref, and the sync useEffect
- Now uses MiniCellGrid (same as MediaTab, ContentTab, StyleTab)
- Section editing and cell subdivision editing are both shown based on
  the selected cell's derived section, no toggle needed
- All structural operations (swap, insert, remove, add/remove
  subdivision) simplified — just update global selectedCell directly

Why: The dual-state system caused sync bugs where ContextBar and other
tabs showed a different cell than the Structure tab. The sync logic was
complex (useEffect with ref guards, manual updates in 5 operations) and
still had edge cases. Using a single source of truth eliminates the
entire category of sync bugs.

Tags: state, layout, structure, ux, cell-selection, refactor
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
